name: Zero Buffer High Dependency
description: There is no independent savings buffer while living support still depends on family.
life_effect: Every transition must be staged because independence and survival are not yet separated.

financial_effect.reason: no savings and family dependency lower financial shock absorption

name: Low Spending Pressure Window
description: Spending pressure is not the main leak; the bigger issue is income path and runway.
life_effect: If income appears, savings can grow faster than in a high-consumption lifestyle.

financial_effect.reason: low spending pressure leaves room for buffer growth once income begins

name: Attention Debt Blocks Saving
description: Attention fragmentation makes money planning inconsistent even when intentions are good.
life_effect: Savings behavior needs automation or simple rules, not more reflection.

financial_effect.reason: fragmented attention makes saving behavior unstable

name: Ladder Saving Ready
description: The person already frames transition as a ladder rather than a leap.
life_effect: A simple cash buffer rule can turn small income into real optionality.

financial_effect.reason: ladder framing supports staged saving and reinvestment
